message:
feat(planner): sqlite_stat1 reads + n_rows in cost model (PLANNER-1+2, PLANNER-3 TODO)

PLANNER-1: wire ANALYZE output back into the planner.

Before this change, ANALYZE wrote to sqlite_stat1 but the stat rows were
never consulted. The cost model always used the HEURISTIC_TABLE_ROWS
default (8192) regardless of reality. Now:

- Add fsqlite_planner::stats::Stat1Summary + parse_stat1(), a pure
  whitespace-separated parser for SQLite's stat format (first integer =
  total rows; trailing integers = per-column rows-per-distinct-value).
- Add Connection::sqlite_stat1_row_counts(), which walks sqlite_stat1
  and returns a tbl-name-lowercased -> n_rows map. A thread-local
  re-entrance guard prevents the SELECT inside this helper from itself
  recursing through the directive/stats path.
- Thread the map through planner_select_directive_with_stats() (new
  variant; the legacy free-fn signature is preserved for the existing
  EXPLAIN call site and unit tests). When an entry is present, the
  PlannerTableStats record gets StatsSource::Analyze and the real row
  count; when absent, heuristics continue as before.

PLANNER-2: n_rows participates in the cost formula.

The old estimate_cost ignored n_rows entirely, so two tables with the
same page count but wildly different row counts ranked identically.
Add estimate_cost_ext(kind, table_pages, index_pages, n_rows) and route
internal call sites through it:

- FullTableScan: page_cost + n_rows * ROW_DECODE_COST
- IndexScanRange: page_cost + selectivity * n_rows * ROW_ACCESS_COST
- IndexScanEquality: page_cost + 1 * ROW_ACCESS_COST (one matched row)
- CoveringIndexScan: page_cost + selectivity * n_rows * ROW_DECODE_COST
  (no table dereference)
- RowidLookup: page_cost + 1 * ROW_ACCESS_COST

estimate_cost() is now a thin wrapper calling estimate_cost_ext() with
n_rows=0, so the two existing public call sites (harness + decision
contract test) continue to see the legacy page-only formulas. Four
internal lib tests that asserted exact page-only costs were updated to
compare against estimate_cost_ext(..., n_rows) instead.

PLANNER-3 (SKIPPED — TODO): stream_hash_join_rows still bypasses the
planner for multi-table FROM. Routing that path through
order_joins_with_hints requires deeper connection.rs surgery and
conflicts with IMPL-28's in-flight work there; leaving to follow-up.

Tests (8 new, all passing):
- planner lib (4): legacy/ext equivalence at n_rows=0, full-scan
  monotonicity in n_rows, range-scan monotonicity, full-vs-index
  preference widening with n_rows.
- planner stats (5): table-only row, index row with per-column counts,
  whitespace tolerance, empty/non-numeric rejection, trailing-garbage.
- core integration (3): ANALYZE round-trip via parse_stat1, multi-table
  row-count separation, malformed-input safety.

All 308 planner tests and the 8-test bd_1as_5 planner correctness
harness pass. cargo clippy + fmt clean on the touched crates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
